sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Joint replacement (primary): hip, knee and shoulder — Recommendations
sources.population: Adults having primary elective hip, knee, or shoulder replacement.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, secondary, and tertiary services before and after elective joint replacement.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for primary elective joint-replacement information and rehabilitation; partial for a generic orthopaedic postoperative workflow and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — shared decisions and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation of postoperative pain, wound care, recovery, rehabilitation, return to work or activity, concerns, questions, and procedure-specific information.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.6 — postoperative rehabilitation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented mobility, activities of daily living, rehabilitation goals, self-directed or supervised rehabilitation, functional impairment, and physiotherapy or occupational-therapy follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.11.1 — long-term care and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports orthopaedic review for new or worsening pain, limp, or loss of function related to a joint replacement while noting the absence of a general fixed monitoring schedule.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG125; last updated 2020-08-19; last reviewed 2023-05-31
sources.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
sources.clinical_setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for postoperative wound-care and surgical-site-infection documentation; partial for traumatic wounds, suture removal, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

exact_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.9–1.4.11 and surgical-site-infection definition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
